title: Data Dictionary for pittsburgh_311.tsv
description:
**Description**

This dataset contains 946 735 municipal service tickets filed by residents and businesses in the City of Pittsburgh from February 2015 through June 2026. Each record is uniquely identified by a composite ID that combines a case number with an internal sequence, and it records the ticket’s status, owning agency, subject category (and legacy code), timestamps for creation, last modification, and closure, as well as address components and geographic coordinates. The tickets are grouped into 9 distinct status values (closed, open, in‑progress, duplicates, etc.), 112 case‑owner agencies, 147 subject categories, and 10 council districts. Geographic data include latitude/longitude pairs with an accuracy flag indicating whether the point is exact or approximate. The dataset is primarily public‑facing service information and contains no personally identifiable or protected health information beyond address components.

---

**Notable Characteristics**

- **Size & Uniqueness** – 946 735 records, with a unique composite ID for every ticket; only ~12 000 case numbers appear twice (≈1.3 % duplicate rate).
- **Status Distribution** – 89 % of tickets are closed, 5 % open, and the remainder in-progress or duplicates/canceled, indicating a highly skewed outcome distribution.
- **Subject & Legacy Subject** – The “Other” category dominates (~47 % of records), while the top ten subjects cover roughly half the data; subject codes mirror this pattern.
- **Geographic Spread** – Latitude ranges from 35.8 to 41.9 °N and longitude from ‑87.6 to ‑75.2 °E, with most points clustered around Pittsburgh’s center (≈60 % exact geocodes, ≈40 % approximate).
- **Temporal Trends** – Creation dates span 11 years but are heavily concentrated in the last few years; modification and closure timestamps show a pronounced spike in 2025–2026.
- **Missingness** – Address fields exhibit high null rates: street (47 %), city (55 %), neighborhood (~3 %); case‑owner missing in ~4 % of records.
- **Data Quality Issues** – Inconsistent status naming (“Closed(Duplicate)” vs “Closed (Duplicate)”), sparse geo‑accuracy labeling, and a large “Other” bucket for categorical fields that may mask meaningful sub‑categories.
- **PII / PHI** – The dataset contains only public address information; no personal identifiers or protected health data are present.

---

_id.title: Record ID
_id.description: A system-generated integer that uniquely identifies each case record. This value serves as the primary key for internal database operations.

case_number.title: Case Number
case_number.description: The alphanumeric identifier issued by the system (e.g., "99-00079204"). It functions as a natural key that can be used to reference or retrieve a specific case.

status.title: Status
status.description: Current state of the case. Common values include "Closed", "Open", "In Progress", and various duplicate or cancellation statuses.

case_owner.title: Case Owner
case_owner.description: The agency responsible for managing the case, such as "DPW - Street Maintenance" or "Police - Zones 1-6".

subject.title: Subject
subject.description: Descriptive category of the issue reported (e.g., "Weeds or Debris", "Potholes", "Snow/Ice Removal").

subject_code.title: Subject Code
subject_code.description: Alphanumeric code representing the subject category (e.g., "WEEDSDEBRIS", "PTHL").

legacy_subject.title: Legacy Subject
legacy_subject.description: Older textual representation of the subject used before the current coding system.

created_date_et.title: Created Date (ET)
created_date_et.description: Timestamp when the case was created, expressed in Eastern Time. This value is equivalent to the UTC timestamp in `created_date_utc` but shifted for local time zone.

created_date_utc.title: Created Date (UTC)
created_date_utc.description: Timestamp when the case was created, expressed in Coordinated Universal Time. This value corresponds to `created_date_et` converted to UTC.

last_modified_date_et.title: Last Modified Date (ET)
last_modified_date_et.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the case, expressed in Eastern Time. Mirrors `last_modified_date_utc` in UTC.

last_modified_date_utc.title: Last Modified Date (UTC)
last_modified_date_utc.description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the case, expressed in Coordinated Universal Time. Mirrors `last_modified_date_et` converted to UTC.

closed_date_et.title: Closed Date (ET)
closed_date_et.description: Timestamp when the case was closed, expressed in Eastern Time. A null value indicates that the case remains open or incomplete.

closed_date_utc.title: Closed Date (UTC)
closed_date_utc.description: Timestamp when the case was closed, expressed in Coordinated Universal Time. Mirrors `closed_date_et` converted to UTC.

origin.title: Origin
origin.description: Source through which the case was submitted (e.g., "Phone", "Web", "API").

street.title: Street Name
street.description: Name of the street where the issue was reported. This is a component of the mailing address; combine with `city`, `neighborhood`, and any ZIP or postal code to form the full address.

street_id.title: Street ID
street_id.description: Surrogate numeric identifier for the street used internally by the system.

city.title: City
city.description: Name of the city in which the case was reported.

neighborhood.title: Neighborhood
neighborhood.description: Local neighborhood or district within the city where the case was reported.

census_tract.title: Census Tract
census_tract.description: Numeric identifier for the census tract covering the case location.

council_district.title: Council District Code
council_district.description: Lettered code indicating the city council district (e.g., "D3").

ward.title: Ward Number
ward.description: Numeric ward number within the city.

police_zone.title: Police Zone Number
police_zone.description: Numeric code for the police zone (1‑6).

latitude.title: Latitude
latitude.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the case location. Together with `longitude`, it defines the geographic point where the issue was reported.

longitude.title: Longitude
longitude.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the case location. Together with `latitude`, it defines the geographic point where the issue was reported.

geo_accuracy.title: Geo Accuracy
geo_accuracy.description: Indicates whether the latitude/longitude pair is exact or approximate.

unique_id.title: Unique ID
unique_id.description: Composite unique identifier that combines the case number and a secondary numeric component to guarantee uniqueness across all records.
